shortTitle: Your Life As The Worst Hitman

content.text: This is you. Cold night, numb fingers clutching a silenced .22 in a rain-soaked alley. You keep replaying the target’s face from the grainy photo. Gray suit. Red scarf. Easy money, they said. All you have to do is wait for the curb light to flick on. In, out, gone before the cops get their donuts. You laugh under your breath—like anything ever goes how you planned.
content.imageDescription: A close-up of a figure standing in a rain-soaked alley at night, breath visible in the cold air. Their gloved hand tightly grasps a small silenced pistol. A crumpled, grainy photo with a face circled—man in gray suit and red scarf—is visible. Puddles reflect dim yellow alley lights, and rain drips off an old dumpster. The figure’s expression is tense but trying to smirk amid the miserable weather.

content.text: You run through the checklist over and over. Bullet in chamber. Gloves tight. Escaped routes burned into your brain like scars. Then, the first shadow appears. Is it him? No—just another drunk, muttering curses at his umbrella. You almost pull the trigger anyway, nerves twitching like live wires. Focus. Don’t choke now.
content.imageDescription: A medium shot of the protagonist in the alley, mentally checking their equipment—a gloved thumb brushes the pistol’s slide, eyes dart toward echoing footsteps. A blurry drunk, staggering under a battered umbrella, lurches out of the darkness past a flickering streetlight. The protagonist’s hand hovers on the trigger, tension visible in their posture as they watch, hesitating.

content.text: You tell yourself you’re invisible. A ghost. Nobody knows who you are. But really, you stick out like a clown in a morgue: one shoe squeaks with every step, the wallet in your pocket pokes your hip because you left your bag in the cab—again.
content.imageDescription: The protagonist, attempting to slink into the shadows, nervously glances down at their feet—one shoe visibly squeaks with each step. A bulging wallet presses obviously against their hip. Nearby windows mirror their awkward, conspicuous presence as they make an effort to blend in but fail.

content.text: You spot the red scarf. Heart lurches. Showtime. The mark walks faster than you expect, half-jogging across puddles. You move after him, keeping low. Suddenly, some street kid on a skateboard rockets past, knocks your arm, gun tumbles into a gutter. You freeze, hope no one saw. Wrong night for luck.
content.imageDescription: From a low angle behind the protagonist, we see the mark in a gray suit and red scarf moving quickly ahead, rain splashing around him. Suddenly, a blur—a teenager on a skateboard—shoots past and collides with the protagonist’s arm, causing the gun to fall from gloved hands and clatter into a nearby gutter. The protagonist freezes, eyes wide, scanning the street in panic.

content.text: You dive for the weapon, ignoring your aching knees. Fingers wrap around cold metal. When you look up, the target’s already twenty yards ahead. You sprint, trying to play it off like you’re just another late commuter, rain flicking in your face. Your pulse deafens you. Even your breath feels too loud for this world.
content.imageDescription: A tense, angled shot captures the protagonist on their knees in the wet street, reaching desperately into a storm drain to retrieve the silenced pistol. Rain slicks their clothes and hands. In the background, the mark is retreating swiftly, back turned. The protagonist clambers up and starts running, attempting to blend with a small crowd of night commuters, face contorted in concentration, breath clouding the air.

content.text: Somehow you catch up at the crosswalk. The light flashes green. You cross, pulling your jacket tight, hand buried in your pocket. Two feet away. This city’s so loud no one notices a thing—except for the one time you wish they wouldn't.
content.imageDescription: A busy city crosswalk just as the signal turns green. The protagonist hunches their shoulders, disguising the pistol in one pocket, as they merge into the crowd right behind the oblivious mark. Neon reflections ripple in puddles underfoot, the city bustling around them, no one paying attention—it’s the perfect chaos.

content.text: You pull the trigger. Click. Nothing. Safety on. In your panic, you fumble, hands shaking. The mark spins, stares at you. His eyes narrow, confusion morphs into alarm. He bolts into the crowd. You give chase—what choice do you have now? Boots slipping, heart pounding, visions of easy money screaming in your skull until they become distant noise.
content.imageDescription: A close-up of the protagonist’s hand concealed in their jacket pocket, squeezing the trigger. There’s an audible ‘click’ and a flash of panic on their face. The mark spins around, eyes meeting the protagonist’s with surprise that turns quickly to fear, and he bursts away into the rushing throng. The protagonist shoves through the crowd after him, urgency in their stride as city lights blur.

content.text: Every step is a slap in the face. You knock into a tourist snapping photos, camera flashes blind you. Apologies spiral in every language. The scarf vanishes behind a food cart. You barrel through, scattering hot dogs and curses, vendors shaking fists at your back. Stomach knots with hunger and dread.
content.imageDescription: An overhead view of the protagonist shoving through a bustling street market. A tourist with a camera accidentally jostles them, camera flash creating white-hot spots in their vision. The mark’s red scarf just disappears behind a busy food cart as the protagonist rushes after him, knocking hot dogs off a counter and inciting angry shouts from vendors. Faces blur by, capturing the chaos and sensory overload.

content.text: Suddenly, you trip over a leash. Some tiny, yapping dog snaps at your ankle. You fall, arms splayed in the oily puddle, shirt ruined, pride in pieces. By the time you stagger to your feet, the mark’s nowhere in sight. You hear sirens—maybe real, maybe just paranoia curling in your head.
content.imageDescription: A comic, almost slapstick low shot: the protagonist’s legs tangle in a barely visible leash, a furious small dog barking and lunging at their ankle. They sprawl face-first into a filthy, reflective puddle. As they stand up, soaked and disheveled, the crowd moves on and the mark is gone. Faint police lights or sirens blur in the distance, adding to the protagonist’s mounting fear.

content.text: You find yourself wandering, rain in your eyes like bitter tears. You mutter every curse you know. Somewhere, a window glows warm with laughter. You remember something—a promise you made to yourself, to never screw up this bad again. But here you are, a soggy, broke hitman, weapon wet and useless, reputation more so.
content.imageDescription: A desolate streetscape. The protagonist shuffles through the rain, hunched and waterlogged, muttering angrily. Across the street, a warm, golden-lit apartment window shows a family laughing, contrasting the protagonist’s isolation. Their pistol hangs uselessly at their side, rain dripping from both weapon and hair.

content.text: You find an alley, press your back against cold brick. You can’t quit, not for one botched job. Maybe tomorrow you’ll find the mark again, maybe you won’t. The street eats men like you for breakfast. But tonight, you’re just another shadow retreating into darkness, badge of failure aching in your pocket.
content.imageDescription: The protagonist stands in a narrow, dark alley, back pressed to rough brick, head bowed. Their shoulders slump in defeat, and the street behind them fades into shadows. One hand rubs a pocket where their wallet—'badge of failure'—presses against their hip, symbolizing regret and resolve. The world outside the alley is blurred and distant.

content.text: You ask yourself: If you can’t even pull off a hit, what the hell are you doing out here? Yet you know—against every ounce of logic—you’ll try again. Because that’s what the worst hitman does. They keep showing up, hoping luck gets tired of laughing at them, even as the city whispers stay down. But you never do.
content.imageDescription: A close-up, introspective shot of the protagonist’s face, outlined by misty breath against the brick. Their expression is a mix of disappointment and stubbornness. The city’s distant neon lights glimmer beyond the alley, as if challenging them, but the protagonist stares forward with new determination, silhouetted by darkness yet refusing to break.

alley.description: Setting (alley): A close-up of a figure standing in a rain-soaked alley at night, breath visible in the cold air. Puddles reflect dim yellow alley lights, and rain drips off an old dumpster. Cold night, numb fingers clutching a silenced .22 in a rain-soaked alley.

car.description: Setting (car): From a low angle behind the protagonist, we see the mark in a gray suit and red scarf moving quickly ahead, rain splashing around him. You spot the red scarf.
